purpose: The mandatory protocol for worker agents whose tasks have upstream dependencies. Before starting implementation, dependent workers MUST read every upstream task's progress file at {tracker_root}/dashboards/{dashboardId}/progress/{dep_id}.json — these files contain the GROUND TRUTH about what actually happened (vs the master's planning-time dispatch prompt which is a stale snapshot). Specifies the four-step protocol: (1) parallel read of all upstream progress files, (2) extraction of status/summary/deviations[]/milestones[]/logs[]/message, (3) approach adaptation based on what was found (especially CRITICAL deviations and upstream failures), (4) logging what was learned plus deviation entries for any adaptations made.
gotchas: Reading upstream progress files is NON-NEGOTIABLE for dependent tasks — the master's dispatch prompt is a stale snapshot from planning time, NOT the actual state of the world after upstream workers ran.

Upstream task with status 'failed' may not block your task — assess whether you can proceed (e.g., the missing artifact wasn't actually critical) and log a 'warn' explaining how you adapted, OR fail your own task with a clear explanation.

CRITICAL severity deviations on upstream tasks ALWAYS require adaptation — your dispatch prompt's assumptions about interfaces/files/APIs may be invalid. Log every adaptation as a deviation in YOUR progress file.
Even 'completed' upstream tasks may have 'error' or 'warn' log entries indicating partial issues — scan logs[] not just status.
Upstream progress files must be read in PARALLEL — they have no dependency on each other and sequential reads waste time.
patterns: Four-step protocol: parallel read → extract critical info → adapt approach → log what you learned (with deviation entries for adaptations).

Field-extraction priority: status (gate), summary (one-line truth), deviations[] with focus on CRITICAL severity, milestones[] for what was actually built, logs[] scanned for error/warn, message for final state.
Adaptation logging convention: log at 'info' level summarizing all upstream reads in one entry, then log each required adaptation as a separate 'deviation' level entry.
conventions: Upstream progress file path schema: {tracker_root}/dashboards/{dashboardId}/progress/{dep_task_id}.json (identical to own progress file path schema).
Multi-dep summary log message format: 'Read upstream dependencies: 1.1 (completed, no deviations), 1.3 (completed, 1 MODERATE deviation — used alternative API pattern)'.
